request.prompt: a danger-zone card whose delete button contains a small confirm checkbox input inside it

findings.message: Required component 'alert-dialog' does not appear in the surface.
findings.rationale: Dialog can be dismissed by clicking the overlay or pressing Escape, so a user can bypass a destructive confirmation without making a conscious choice. AlertDialog forces an explicit confirm/cancel decision and is announced with greater urgency by screen readers.
Nested interactive elements create ambiguous click targets and are an accessibility violation: screen readers cannot determine intent and click handling varies across browsers.

children.text: This will permanently delete your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone.

findings.message: 'button' must carry non-empty direct text (its own `text` field — text in descendants does not count).
findings.rationale: The trigger button must present its label as its own text. Assistive technology reads the control's accessible name from the element itself, and protocol projections lift a trigger label from the label-bearing component's direct text — a label nested in a child (e.g. a badge) or absent entirely produces a control with no accessible name and an instance downstream emitters must refuse.

findings.message: Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-cancel' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
findings.rationale: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.

findings.message: Required sub-component 'alert-dialog-title' (min 1) not found among descendants (found 0).
findings.rationale: A confirmation without an explicit cancel action and a title naming the consequence funnels the user toward the destructive action; the title is also required for aria-labelledby.

findings.message: 'button' must carry non-empty direct text (its own `text` field — text in descendants does not count).
findings.rationale: The trigger button must present its label as its own text. Assistive technology reads the control's accessible name from the element itself, and protocol projections lift a trigger label from the label-bearing component's direct text — a label nested in a child (e.g. a badge) or absent entirely produces a control with no accessible name and an instance downstream emitters must refuse.

repairMessages:
Your surface violates 2 governance rule finding(s) of the "shadcn/ui" design system:

Violation 1: [rule.destructive-requires-alertdialog / must]
  At: $.root (component: surface)
  Required component 'alert-dialog' does not appear in the surface.
  Why: Dialog can be dismissed by clicking the overlay or pressing Escape, so a user can bypass a destructive confirmation without making a conscious choice. AlertDialog forces an explicit confirm/cancel decision and is announced with greater urgency by screen readers.
Violation 2: [rule.button-no-interactive-descendants / must]
  At: $.root.children[1].children[1].children[0].children[0] (component: input)
  Forbidden descendant 'input' inside 'button' (at $.root.children[1].children[1]).
  Why: Nested interactive elements create ambiguous click targets and are an accessibility violation: screen readers cannot determine intent and click handling varies across browsers.

Respond with a single JSON object conforming to the provided schema. No commentary.
